codeReview.summary: Well-structured implementation that correctly parses SSE token usage for all 7 UpstreamMode values. The approach is sound: extractUsageCountsFromSseText is a pure, exported function dispatching to four dedicated parsers (OpenAI Chat, Anthropic, Responses API, Ollama). Uses response.clone().text() to preserve the original response body for downstream handlers. Defense-in-depth with nested try/catch ensures the function never throws. All existing non-streaming code paths are completely unchanged (verified via diff). The 11 new unit tests provide good coverage across all modes plus graceful degradation for empty, missing, and malformed input.

issues.description: response.clone().text() buffers the entire stream body a second time in memory. For very large streaming responses this doubles memory usage. Acceptable given the proxy already buffers full streams (e.g., line 1229 in ResponsesProviderStrategy), but worth noting for future optimization if memory pressure becomes an issue.

In extractUsageFromOllamaNdjson, if the final done:true chunk has prompt_eval_count but not eval_count (or vice versa), usage is silently dropped (returns {}). This is a reasonable design choice since Ollama always sends both together, but it means partial Ollama usage data is never surfaced — unlike the Anthropic parser which handles partial usage (input_tokens only).

sharedStateObservations.observation: The architecture.md library doc already documents the token data flow (extractUsageCounts → updateUsageCountsFromResponse → RequestLogStore.update → buildUsageOverview → Dashboard). The streaming SSE parsing addition fits cleanly into this documented flow with no architectural changes.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md last bullet: 'Data flow for tokens: API response → extractUsageCounts() → updateUsageCountsFromResponse() → RequestLogStore.update() → buildUsageOverview() → Dashboard API → React components.'

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker correctly followed the project convention of using node:test with node:assert/strict for the new test file, matching the existing test infrastructure. Test file placement in src/tests/ is consistent with project structure.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/tests/sse-usage.test.ts uses 'import test from "node:test"' and 'import assert from "node:assert/strict"', matching src/tests/proxy.test.ts patterns.

summary: PASS. The implementation correctly fixes the SSE token usage extraction bug across all provider formats (OpenAI Chat, Anthropic Messages, OpenAI Responses API, Ollama). Code is well-structured with a clean separation into a testable pure function (extractUsageCountsFromSseText) and four format-specific parsers. The response.clone() approach correctly preserves the original response body for downstream handlers. Error handling is thorough with defense-in-depth try/catch. 11 new tests cover all 7 UpstreamMode values plus graceful degradation. Non-streaming code path is untouched. Two non-blocking observations noted: memory doubling from clone and asymmetric partial-usage handling between Ollama and Anthropic parsers.
